name: Tilapia En Papillote with Asparagus & Shrimp
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
4 large tilapia filets (1 1/4 to 1 1/2 pounds total)
2 oz Tiny cooked shrimp rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Snap off the thick ends of the asparagus, cut the spears diagonally into bite-size pieces, and blanch in lightly salted water. Drain and cool.
2. Beat the butter until light, then blend in the lemon zest and season with salt and pepper to taste.
3. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C).
4. Fold a 12-inch square of baking parchment in half diagonally and crease the corners.
5. Arrange one tilapia filet on one side of the paper, season with a little lemon juice, and top with a quarter of the shrimp and asparagus.
6. Dot with a quarter of the lemon butter. Fold the other side of the paper over the fish and seal the package into a half oval shape with a series of creases, starting with one corner and finishing with a twist at the opposite end.
7. Repeat for the remaining portions.
8. Bake the packages on a sheet pan until the parchment is browned and puffy, 7 to 8 minutes.
9. Serve by opening each package and sliding the contents onto a plate, or serve in the paper and let each diner cut open their own portion.
